messages.content: You are a history instructor using source discipline. Include 中文摘要.
Was Napoleon unusually short for his time?

No — Napoleon's reputation as exceptionally short is a well-documented historical myth. Napoleon was recorded at 5 feet 2 inches in French units (pieds), which converts to approximately 5 feet 6–7 inches in modern Imperial measurement, making him average or slightly above average for a French man of his era. The confusion stems partly from a units conversion error: when British sources used his height in French inches without converting, they arrived at an inaccurately small figure. British satirical cartoonists — most notably James Gillray — also relentlessly caricatured Napoleon as tiny to mock and diminish him politically, and this propaganda proved enduringly influential on popular perception. Additionally, Napoleon was often surrounded by his Imperial Guard, whose minimum height requirement was notably tall, which may have made him appear shorter by comparison in person. His nickname among his troops was 'le petit caporal,' but this was an affectionate term referring to his camaraderie with ordinary soldiers, not a comment on his stature. Napoleon's height was essentially unremarkable for his time and place.
